question: Per the routing-events best-practice, which intermediate event type is currently unsupported for execution in Camunda 8?

a.text: The intermediate-events comparison table marks the conditional event as 'Not yet supported in Camunda 8', recommending that you determine the condition externally yourself and use a message event instead.

b.text: Intermediate message catch events are fully supported and are the recommended way to route an incoming message to a specific, unique process instance via a correlation key.

c.text: Intermediate timer events are supported and recommended whenever a process instance must wait for a point in time or a duration.

d.text: Intermediate signal catch events are supported and are recommended when an incoming signal needs to be routed to all process instances waiting for it.

explanation: Message, timer, signal and receive-task intermediate elements are all supported. The conditional event is the outlier — the docs say it is not yet supported in Camunda 8, so the recommended workaround is to determine the condition externally yourself and drive the process forward with a message event.

docs.title: Routing events to processes - best practices
